summary: EXHAUSTIVE every-element enumeration audit of Crafty (keep/improve/delete/rebuild + dead-legacy-error sweep) -> feeds the comprehensive master plan

audits.category: A — Boot & Shell & Menus (front door + all chrome states)
audits.summary: The boot/shell layer is functional but carries the exact REACTIVE-cleanup failures Kevin flagged: the legacy purple "CRAFTY RPG" click-to-play overlay still ships to prod (App.jsx:759), and the `gameStarted` flag is read in 5 places yet was NEVER defined or set in the store in any commit — it is permanently `undefined`, which silently DEADENS the ESC=PAUSE feature (App.jsx:208-213) and 3 mid-game pointer-relock guards (MenuSystem.jsx:198/220/230). A whole auth subsystem (AuthContext + AuthModal + axios) ships dead: it fires a doomed `axios.get(http://localhost:8001/api/auth/me)` on EVERY prod boot, gating the entire app behind the "Loading Crafty…" spinner until that network call fails, and the AuthModal is reachable ONLY via a DEV test hook (no production button opens it). The title screen is the "childish" surface Kevin named — flat radial-gradient + 2D drifting "confetti" squares + off-brand Orbitron-shimmer wordmark + candy-purple glow CTA — far below a SOTA-June-2026 title bar (live 3D scene backdrop, depth, brand-consistent type). Plus boot-chrome gaps: no favicon, no manifest, no OG meta, "Minecraft-style" trademark-risk copy, and 2 dead CSS keyframes.
topPriorities: BUG: `gameStarted` is undefined everywhere (never in store) — ESC=PAUSE is dead AND 3 mid-game pointer-relock guards never fire. Either add a real `gameStarted` store flag set on first pointer-lock, OR replace the guard with `isWorldBuilt`/`isAlive`. XS effort, high impact — this is the exact dead-flag class Kevin called out.

DELETE/REBUILD: the legacy purple 'CRAFTY RPG' click-to-play overlay (App.jsx:734-806) — off-brand chrome (slate/blue glass, NOT the locked bold-flat system) that ships to prod and duplicates the title menu's control hints. This is the 'CRAFTY RPG flash' surface. S effort.

DEAD_CODE: the entire auth path ships dead AND harmful — AuthContext fires axios GET to http://localhost:8001 on every prod boot (no VITE_BACKEND_URL exists), blocking boot behind the Loading spinner until it fails + logging a console error; AuthModal is opened only by a DEV test hook. Delete AuthProvider/AuthModal/AuthContext or gate the auth check so it never runs in prod. M effort, high impact (boot latency + console error every load).

REBUILD: the title screen look — flat gradient + 2D confetti squares + Orbitron-shimmer wordmark + candy-purple glow button is the 'childish' surface; SOTA-June-2026 wants a live 3D scene backdrop with depth + brand-consistent (Lilita One / bold-flat) type and a bold-flat CTA. The 3D TitleMascot is the one keeper. L effort, high impact (first impression).

DELETE: dead CSS in App.css — `float-slow` + `title-glow` keyframes have zero consumers; the `Orbitron` Google-Fonts @import is a render-blocking external request used only by the childish title wordmark + one tiny HUD canvas label. XS effort.
deadOrLegacyOrError: frontend/src/App.jsx:210 — `s.gameStarted` is always undefined (flag never defined/set in store) → ESC-to-pause settings branch is unreachable (the very bug commit ca7f86e claimed to fix)
frontend/src/App.jsx:203 — comment 'pre-game is suppressed (gameStarted)' documents a guard that does nothing
frontend/src/MenuSystem.jsx:198 — `gameState.gameStarted &&` relock guard (WorldManager close) never fires → pointer never re-locks
frontend/src/MenuSystem.jsx:220 — same dead guard on TradingInterface close
frontend/src/MenuSystem.jsx:230 — same dead guard on AuthModal close
frontend/src/App.jsx:734-806 — legacy 'CRAFTY RPG' purple/slate-glass click-to-play overlay (off-brand, not bold-flat, ships to prod)
frontend/src/App.jsx:759 — literal 'CRAFTY RPG' heading (the only one left in src)
frontend/src/AuthContext.jsx:36 — axios.get('http://localhost:8001/api/auth/me') fires on EVERY prod boot; no VITE_BACKEND_URL env exists → guaranteed failed network request + console.error per load
frontend/src/AuthContext.jsx:17,42 — `loading:true` until the doomed auth call rejects → App.jsx:694 blocks the whole app behind 'Loading Crafty…' until network timeout
frontend/src/AuthComponents.jsx (whole AuthModal) — only opened by the DEV-only `openAuth` test hook (App.jsx:627-629); no production UI opens it → dead in prod
frontend/src/MenuSystem.jsx:41 — `isAuthenticated` prop destructured but never used in the component body (dead prop; also passed from App.jsx:845)

frontend/src/App.css title-glow keyframe — zero consumers

frontend/index.html — no <link rel=icon> (default browser globe favicon ships), no PWA manifest, no Open Graph/social meta
frontend/index.html meta description — 'Minecraft-style' is a trademark-risk + off-brand phrasing in the shipped <head>

elements.name: frontend/index.html (Vite entry / shipped <head>)
elements.state: Minimal head: charset, viewport, description='A magical Minecraft-style crafting game…', title='Crafty | Magical Adventure', #root div, module script. Build/index.html confirms this is exactly what ships (plus injected asset preloads). NO favicon link, NO manifest, NO OG/Twitter meta, NO theme-color.
elements.recommendation: Add a favicon (currently the default browser globe ships), a web manifest + apple-touch-icon (PWA/installable, matters for the iPad/iPhone target), Open Graph + Twitter card meta (share previews), and theme-color. Replace 'Minecraft-style' (trademark-risk, off-brand) with brand-accurate copy.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA web games ship a branded favicon, installable PWA manifest, and rich share-card meta; Crafty ships none — a 2024-tier bare entry.

elements.name: frontend/src/index.jsx — global console/error capture + ReactDOM root + ErrorBoundary
elements.state: Patches console.log/warn/error + window.onerror/onunhandledrejection into a ring buffer (window.__debugLogs, cap 100) — RUNS IN PROD (no DEV gate), feeding DebugOverlay (which is DEV-gated). Mounts <ErrorBoundary><App/></ErrorBoundary>. ErrorBoundary is a self-contained bold-flat crash screen with inline hex (crash-safe), DEV-only component-stack.
elements.recommendation: ErrorBoundary itself = KEEP (well-built, crash-safe, DEV-gated stack). But the console-monkeypatch ring buffer runs in PROD for no prod consumer (DebugOverlay is DEV-only) — gate the whole __debugLogs patch behind import.meta.env.DEV to stop overriding console in production.
elements.sotaGap: Prod builds should not monkeypatch console; SOTA routes prod errors to a real reporter (Sentry-class) or nothing, not a dev ring buffer.

elements.name: ErrorBoundary (in index.jsx)
elements.state: Bold-flat last-resort crash screen, self-contained inline styles (palette hex, not tokens — correct, since a crash can predate applyThemeVars), DEV-only raw component stack, Reload button. On-brand.
elements.recommendation: Keep as-is. Good defensive design.

elements.name: App.jsx top-level loading screen (the 'Loading Crafty…' gate)
elements.state: App.jsx:694 — `if (loading) return <spinner>Loading Crafty…</spinner>`. `loading` comes from AuthContext and is true until the doomed localhost:8001 auth call rejects. So EVERY prod boot shows this spinner until a network request fails, THEN proceeds. Flat blue gradient, generic spinner.
elements.recommendation: Root cause = the dead auth check (see AuthContext). Once auth is removed/gated, this gate should key off real boot readiness (world/asset load), not a network failure. Also restyle to bold-flat brand (currently generic blue gradient).
elements.sotaGap: SOTA boots fast with a branded loader tied to real asset progress; Crafty gates boot on a network request that is GUARANTEED to fail in prod.

elements.name: GENERATING WORLD overlay (App.jsx:724-732)
elements.state: Shown while !isWorldBuilt (until isSpawnChunkLoaded). Green-mono 'GENERATING WORLD' + 'Building Terrain and Physics Colliders…', spinning ring, glow text-shadow. Functional, real, gates on a real signal.
elements.recommendation: Keep the gate (real), but the green-terminal-mono aesthetic is off-brand vs the locked bold-flat amber/ink system. Restyle to the brand + consider a determinate progress bar instead of an indeterminate spinner.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA voxel loaders show determinate chunk-load progress + branded art; this is a generic spinner with hacker-green type that clashes with the in-game UI.

elements.name: Click-to-play overlay 'CRAFTY RPG' (App.jsx:734-806)
elements.state: LEGACY surface Kevin flagged. Slate-950/blue-glass card, blurred backdrop, blue gradient 'CRAFTY RPG' heading, play-triangle SVG, 'Quick Controls' grid (W A S D / Left-Click / etc.). Shows when isWorldBuilt && !pointerLocked && !panelOpen && alive. NOT built on the bold-flat Panel/Button primitives — raw Tailwind slate/blue palette. Duplicates the control hints already on the title menu.
elements.recommendation: This is the 'CRAFTY RPG' surface. Either DELETE (the title menu already covers re-entry + control hints) or REBUILD on the bold-flat primitives with brand-correct copy ('Crafty', not 'CRAFTY RPG'). It overlaps the title menu's role — decide ONE re-entry surface.
elements.sotaGap: Two competing re-entry surfaces (this glass card + the title menu) with inconsistent branding; SOTA has ONE coherent click-to-resume in the game's design language.

elements.name: Title/landing menu (MenuSystem.jsx titleMenuVisible block, lines 234-326)
elements.state: Shows when unlocked + alive + no panel (via shouldShowTitleMenu). Flat radial-gradient bg (#1a1040→#050510), 40 twinkling stars + 15 drifting colored 'confetti' squares (menu-particle), live 3D TitleMascot (Suspense→2D Icon fallback), Orbitron-shimmer 'Crafty' h1 (text-8xl, gold gradient shimmer), purple 'Build • Craft • Cast Spells • Explore Infinite Worlds' tagline (wraps awkwardly), candy-purple glow-pulse 'Start Adventure' button, control-hint row. Verified live (baseline menu.png).
elements.recommendation: This is the 'childish' screen. REBUILD to SOTA: replace flat gradient + 2D confetti with a live 3D scene backdrop (a render of the actual voxel world / hero scene with depth + ambient light); swap Orbitron-shimmer wordmark for the brand display face (Lilita One / bold-flat); replace candy-purple glow CTA with a bold-flat ink-framed amber CTA; fix the tagline line-wrap. KEEP the 3D TitleMascot (the one SOTA-leaning element).
elements.sotaGap: worldofclaudecraft.com-class title screens use a cinematic live 3D backdrop + brand-consistent type + restrained motion. Crafty's is a flat 2D gradient with arcade-shimmer type + drifting candy squares — reads as a 2010s flash-game splash.

elements.name: TitleMascot (live 3D 'Crafty Hero' on the menu)
elements.state: Lazy + Suspense-wrapped R3F voxel wizard, 2D Icon fallback until canvas mounts, idle bob frozen in capture mode for deterministic baselines. On-brand, real, SOTA-leaning. Verified in baseline (the voxel wizard).
elements.recommendation: Keep. This is the strongest element of the title screen; build the rebuilt title backdrop around it.

elements.name: gameStarted flag (DEAD — read in 5 places, never defined/set)
elements.state: Read at App.jsx:210 and MenuSystem.jsx:198/220/230. Confirmed via git history (git log -S) it was NEVER in the store in any commit — introduced as a guard ref in commits ca7f86e + 4cf1717 without the backing flag. Permanently undefined. Consequence: (1) App.jsx:208-213 ESC=PAUSE branch never opens settings (the exact failure that commit claimed to fix); (2) the 3 MenuSystem relock guards never re-acquire pointer lock when closing WorldManager/Trading/Auth mid-game → player lands unlocked.
elements.recommendation: Either add a real `gameStarted` boolean to useGameStore set true on first successful pointer-lock (or on isWorldBuilt+first enterPlay), OR replace each guard with an existing truthy signal (isWorldBuilt / isAlive). Then re-verify ESC opens the pause menu and mid-game panel-close re-locks. Add a unit/static gate asserting every store-flag read in App/MenuSystem resolves to a defined store key.
elements.sotaGap: A core 'press ESC to pause' is silently broken — basic shell completeness a SOTA game gets right; this is exactly the dead-flag class Kevin said survived prior passes.

elements.name: Pause flow / SettingsPanel-as-pause (App.jsx:204-215 ESC transition)
elements.state: Intent: on pointer-unlock true→false transition, if alive+gameStarted+no-panel → open SettingsPanel as the pause menu. But gameStarted===undefined kills the branch (see above). So ESC actually just unlocks → falls through to the title menu (shouldShowTitleMenu = unlocked+alive+no-panel). 'ESC = pause' is effectively 'ESC = back to title menu'.
elements.recommendation: Fix is the gameStarted fix above. Once fixed, verify the intended behavior: ESC mid-game opens SettingsPanel (pause), NOT the title menu. Confirm the title menu and pause settings don't both qualify in the same unlocked state.
elements.sotaGap: Players expect ESC→pause with resume/settings/quit; current behavior dumps them to the marketing title screen mid-run.

elements.name: shouldShowTitleMenu / isAnyPanelOpen / PANEL_FLAGS (panelState.js)
elements.state: Single source of truth for 'is a panel open' + 'should the title menu render'. Pure, unit-tested, consolidates a previously-drifting 13-panel list. Documents its own history (the 2026-06-07 U-key menu-over-panel bug). Well-built. Notes a durable follow-up: move the 4 React-local flags (showSpellUpgrades/showAchievements/showStats/showAuthModal) into the store so one selector suffices.
elements.recommendation: Keep. Good architecture. Optionally execute the documented follow-up (move the 4 React-local flags into the store) to remove the per-caller merge ritual — but only if showAuthModal survives the auth-deletion decision.

elements.name: AuthContext / AuthProvider (axios sessions)
elements.state: Real implementation: on boot fires axios.get(http://localhost:8001/api/auth/me); login/register/logout POST to localhost:8001. No VITE_BACKEND_URL env exists anywhere → prod always hits localhost:8001 → guaranteed failure. `loading` stays true until that rejects, gating the whole app. Consumers: App (isAuthenticated unused beyond destructure; loading gates boot), AuthModal (login/register), WorldManager (user for world ownership/naming).
elements.recommendation: This subsystem is dead-in-prod AND actively harmful (boot latency + console error every load). DECIDE with Kevin: (a) DELETE auth entirely (AuthContext+AuthModal+the WorldManager `user` branches → 'Guest' only), or (b) if multiplayer/accounts are roadmap, gate the boot auth check so it NEVER blocks/runs without a configured backend and only attempts when VITE_BACKEND_URL is set. Default recommendation: DELETE — no production path reaches login.
elements.sotaGap: Shipping a dead auth client that fails a network call on every boot is a defect; SOTA either has working accounts or no auth client at all.

elements.name: AuthModal (AuthComponents.jsx)
elements.state: Bold-flat rebuilt (M6 #17) login/register modal on Panel/Button/Toast primitives — visually on-brand and well-made. BUT: opened ONLY by the DEV-only `openAuth` test hook (App.jsx:627-629). The code comment claims it's 'reachable from the title menu' — FALSE: no button anywhere in MenuSystem/HUD/title calls setShowAuthModal(true). Unreachable in production.
elements.recommendation: Tied to the AuthContext decision. If auth is deleted → delete AuthModal too. If kept → either add a real 'Sign In' entry point on the title menu (and fix the backend URL), or keep as DEV-only and remove the false 'reachable from the title menu' comment.
elements.sotaGap: A polished modal that no player can open is wasted surface + a misleading comment.

elements.name: isAuthenticated prop on MenuSystem
elements.state: Passed App.jsx:845 → destructured MenuSystem.jsx:41 → never referenced in the component body.
elements.recommendation: Remove the prop from both the call site and the destructure.

elements.name: CreditsScreen (ui/CreditsScreen.jsx)
elements.state: Bold-flat Modal, tokens-only, no emoji. Attributes game-icons.net (CC BY 3.0, owed) + fonts (OFL / free-commercial) + 'built with'. i18n via t(). Reached from SettingsPanel→onOpenCredits. Real, on-brand, legally necessary.
elements.recommendation: Keep. Satisfies the CC BY 3.0 attribution mandate. Minor: verify all i18n keys (credits.*) resolve in both en + zh-CN.

elements.name: DeathScreen (GameSystems.jsx:211)
elements.state: Bold-flat overlay, 'YOU DIED' danger title, run summary (Level + Nights survived), Respawn button that re-acquires pointer lock. On-brand, real, mounts on death.
elements.recommendation: Keep. Solid. Optional polish: a SOTA death screen adds more run stats (kills, time survived, depth) + a subtle vignette/sting — but functionally complete and on-brand.
elements.sotaGap: Minor — SOTA death summaries are richer (full run stats, leaderboard hook); Crafty's is minimal but correct.

elements.name: VictoryOverlay (GameSystems.jsx:241)
elements.state: Bold-flat 'VICTORY' (warn/amber), fires window.playVictory sting on mount, run summary, 'Keep exploring' dismiss (post-climax endless handoff). Gated on bossSystem.bossDefeated && !victoryDismissed. On-brand, real.
elements.recommendation: Keep. Good win-state beat. Same optional polish note as DeathScreen (richer stats / more cinematic climax).
elements.sotaGap: Minor — a SOTA win screen is more cinematic (credits roll hook, stats, share); functionally complete here.

elements.name: App.css — title-menu CSS (keyframes + classes)
elements.state: Contains: shake (USED, combat), float (USED, menu wrapper bob), float-slow (DEAD — 0 consumers), shimmer+shimmer-text (USED, title wordmark), glow-pulse+glow-button (USED, title CTA — deliberately excluded from the single-UI gate per S1-C-M2c), title-glow (DEAD — 0 consumers), star-twinkle+menu-star (USED), drift-left/right+menu-particle (USED, the 'confetti'). Line 1: render-blocking Google-Fonts @import of Orbitron (used only by .pixel-font title wordmark + one 9px HUD canvas label).
elements.recommendation: DELETE the 2 dead keyframes (float-slow, title-glow). Self-host or drop the render-blocking Orbitron @import. The menu-particle 'confetti' + shimmer/glow classes survive only if the childish title screen survives — they should be removed in the title-screen REBUILD.
elements.sotaGap: Render-blocking external Google-Fonts @import hurts boot LCP; SOTA self-hosts/preloads fonts. The decorative classes encode the off-brand title aesthetic.

elements.name: GameMethods.js (cross-system imperative registry)
elements.state: Tiny 211-byte module exporting `GameMethods = {}` — a global registry populated at import-time by NPC/Terrain/Magic/Quest systems (damageMob, castBuildRay, grantXP, spawnLootDrop, etc.). Heavily used across ~15 files. Real and load-bearing (Game-Loop-Isolation pattern: keeps imperative fns off zustand).
elements.recommendation: Keep — this is core architecture, not chrome (included for completeness since it's imported by App.jsx). Not a menu/boot concern.

audits.category: B — WORLD (terrain gen, biomes, ocean/water, sky/skydome, atmosphere, fog, lighting, postprocessing, weather)
audits.summary: The WORLD layer is technically solid and dead-code-light: the greedy mesher, DataArrayTexture atlas, vertex AO, shared single-source height formula (heightAt.js), ore-by-depth, deterministic foliage/landmarks, and the mood-driven Atmosphere/sky/fog/postproc are all real, wired, and capture-deterministic — no orphaned files in this category. The two big honest gaps are PERCEPTUAL, not structural: (1) the spawn reads as a peak on a tiny island floating in a flat blue void (OCEAN_CONTINENT_THRESHOLD=-0.15 keeps the coastline pinned right at spawn; Hearth pad at y56 over a ~y50 grade sits the player ABOVE the surrounding terrain), and (2) water renders as a CHECKERBOARD of square voxel tops (a per-column color-grade on un-merged 1x1 top quads), NOT a continuous water surface — verified directly in baseline frames ocean-coast.png / explore-day.png / hearth.png. Lighting is flat (one directional sun + flat ambient, NO hemisphere light; the fill pointLight is OFF in daytime fillIntensity:0). Weather is a global biome-agnostic clear→rain→snow loop every 90s with no audio/lightning/wet-surface tie-in — purely cosmetic. The dusk/night sky + underwater god-ray scene are the standout assets. NOTE: the prompt's two named dead-code items (the dead `gameStarted` flag and the legacy "CRAFTY RPG" click-to-play overlay) live in App.jsx/MenuSystem.jsx — category A/HUD, not WORLD — but I confirmed `gameStarted` is read in 5 sites and SET nowhere (always falsy).

elements.name: heightAt.js — shared surface formula (single source of truth)
elements.state: frontend/src/world/heightAt.js. Pure computeHeight(noise2D,x,z) returning {continent,moisture,temperature,n,baseHeight}. baseHeight = 40 + n*18 + highland^2*90, highland fires only where low-freq noise > 0.62 (~4.4% of columns). Imported by BOTH terrain.worker.js and climate.js so they cannot drift (a real past drift bug is documented in the header). S4 'tame-the-mountains' tuning applied.
elements.recommendation: Keep. The single-source design is correct and the de-mountaining is sound. Future: the relief is now quite GENTLE (max highland relief +12.8) which contributes to the flat-island read — pair any de-island work (lowering OCEAN_CONTINENT_THRESHOLD) with a modest relief/biome-scale increase so the larger landmass doesn't read as a uniform green plain.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA voxel worlds (worldofclaudecraft, Minecraft 1.18+) use multi-octave continentalness/erosion/peaks-and-valleys spline noise for dramatic-yet-traversable terrain; this is a single highland-swell term on a low-amplitude base — readable but low drama.

elements.name: terrain.worker.js — chunk gen + greedy mesher
elements.state: frontend/src/world/terrain.worker.js (840 LOC). Generates 16x16x256 chunks: surface formula -> ocean/land branch -> biome block -> beach override -> 3D swiss-cheese caves + cellular automata + dungeon blueprint stamping + cavern support beams + foliage (trees/cacti/pines) + Hearth stamp + player-mod replay. Greedy mesher sweeps 3 axes, merges faces, bakes vertexColor(r=blockType, g=foam, b=depth) + AO + UVs. Water-top faces deliberately NOT merged (per-column foam/depth). Transfers buffers to main thread.
elements.recommendation: Keep — this is real load-bearing engine code. Two minor notes: (a) the cross-chunk shoreline-foam seam is a known minor gap (getBlock returns 0 across chunk edges so foam can miss at chunk borders); (b) caves/dungeons/support-beams are generated but it's unclear how reachable/visible they are in normal play (separate audit). No dead code.
elements.sotaGap: Mesher + atlas are genuinely SOTA-grade for a web voxel game. Gap is content density, not the pipeline.

elements.name: biomeTable.js — biome selection (3 biomes)
elements.state: frontend/src/world/biomeTable.js. Only THREE biomes: desert (temp>0.7 && moisture<0.3), snow (temp<0.3), plains (everything else). `continent` param is accepted but IGNORED (dead param, documented as 'for M4's use'). Byte-identical to the old inline branch.
elements.recommendation: Expand the biome table. 3 biomes (and one of them — plains — is the default catch-all that dominates the map) is the single biggest CONTENT gap in the world. Add forest/jungle/savanna/swamp/taiga/mesa with distinct surface+secondary blocks, foliage, and tint. The `continent` unused param signals the table was built to grow and never did.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA voxel worlds ship 10-60 biomes with smooth temp/humidity blends and per-biome flora/color/structures; Crafty has 3 hard-threshold biomes with no blending — the world reads same-y everywhere.

elements.name: oceanProfile.js — ocean profile + foam/depth kernels
elements.state: frontend/src/world/oceanProfile.js. SEA_LEVEL=28, BEACH_BAND_TOP=30, DEEP_FLOOR=6 (max divable depth 22). oceanBlend lerps land->seabed over continent in [-0.30,-0.15]. shoreFoamFactor + seabedDepthT are pure kernels consumed by the mesher. Header EXPLICITLY DEFERS the de-island fix: 'OCEAN_CONTINENT_THRESHOLD stays at -0.15 until S4b' — S4b was never done.
elements.recommendation: Execute the deferred S4b: lower OCEAN_CONTINENT_THRESHOLD so the coastline is pushed far from spawn and the player no longer feels marooned on a peak-island. Per the header this requires relocating the ocean-depth/ocean-coast capture cameras (pinned to the old -0.15 shore) — a known, scoped task. This is THE fix for the 'seemingly on an island' complaint.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: oceans are vast continuous bodies with the player on a large continent; here the sea laps right up to the spawn pad on all sides.

elements.name: Water RENDERING — checkerboard voxel tops, not a surface
elements.state: frontend/src/world/Terrain.jsx compileShader + terrain.worker.js water-top branch. Water is voxel blocks (id 9, alpha 0.75). Top faces are NOT greedy-merged: each is a 1x1 quad carrying per-column foam(g)+depth(b). Shader does depth-tint navy + shallow-teal lift + per-column depth grade + Fresnel sheen + shore foam + night bioluminescence + a small sin/cos vertex wave (±0.12). VERIFIED in ocean-coast.png: the surface reads as a hard checkerboard of teal/navy squares with a stairstep coastline, NOT a flowing water plane.
elements.recommendation: This is the #1 world visual defect Kevin called out. The per-column depth grade makes adjacent water tops DIFFERENT colors -> a literal grid. Either (a) blend the depth factor across neighbors (smooth the color so it reads as one body), or (b) render water as a single flat animated plane mesh at SEA_LEVEL with a proper water shader (normal-mapped ripples / screen-space reflection / refraction) instead of per-voxel tops. SOTA web water = a continuous surface; voxel-cube water tops are the look the prompt explicitly flags.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA (worldofclaudecraft, stylized R3F water): continuous reflective/refractive surface with animated normals + foam lines + caustics. Crafty: alpha cubes with per-column flat-color grid + a tiny vertex bob.

elements.name: Underwater scene (depth tint + god-rays + biolum)
elements.state: Visible in ocean-depth.png. Below the surface the depth gradient (navy deepening), god-ray shafts, and night bioluminescence read GENUINELY well — the strongest single WORLD asset. Seabed is hard cubes (DEEP_FLOOR=6..10) but the volumetric feel works.
elements.recommendation: Keep. This is the one place the voxel-water look pays off (caustic-like shafts + glow). Do NOT regress it when fixing the surface checkerboard — re-pin the ocean-depth capture after any threshold change.
elements.sotaGap: Close to SOTA for the underwater read; minor gap is the blocky seabed and lack of suspended-particle drift.

elements.name: Atmosphere.jsx — lights (sun + ambient + fill, NO hemisphere)
elements.state: frontend/src/render/Atmosphere.jsx. Exactly ONE directionalLight (sun, castShadow, intensity 1.5 base, mood-driven), ONE flat ambientLight (0.6 base), ONE pointLight fill at FIXED [0,20,0] that is OFF in daytime (explore fillIntensity:0). NO hemisphereLight. Sun position is mood-driven but the directional shadow cam is fixed ±100.
elements.recommendation: Add a HemisphereLight (sky-color top / ground-color bottom) — this is the cheapest, highest-impact fix for the flat-look: it gives terrain a sky-vs-ground ambient gradient so faces read with form instead of uniform flat green. Currently the only directional cue is the single sun + the baked vertex AO; flat ambient washes everything to one value. The daytime fill being 0 means explore has NO fill at all.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA outdoor lighting = directional sun + hemisphere ambient (or IBL/env map) so surfaces get directional sky tint; Crafty has flat constant ambient -> the 'flat-look' the prompt names.

elements.name: Atmosphere.jsx — gradient skydome + sun glow + twilight stars/moon
elements.state: frontend/src/render/Atmosphere.jsx makeSkyDomeMaterial. Custom 3-stop gradient dome (horizon->mid->top) + warm sun glow + sun disc + uStar-gated twilight star field + a cool moon disc (dusk-only). Follows camera, depthTest/Write off, fog off. VERIFIED beautiful in explore-night.png (orange dusk gradient + moon).
elements.recommendation: Keep — the sky is a genuine asset, especially at dusk. Minor: the day sky (explore-day.png) is a clean blue gradient but reads slightly empty (no clouds). Optional: add subtle drifting cloud billboards or a horizon haze band for daytime richness.

audits.summary: The input ARCHITECTURE is genuinely strong: a clean pure-JS intent SoT (inputState.js), a unit-tested verb router, pure look/locomotion/dodge math, a custom lenient pointer-look (replacing fragile drei PLC), and a fully-built touch layer — 85 input/dodge unit tests pass. But Kevin's two flagged ghosts are REAL and STILL LIVE: (1) `gameStarted` is referenced in 5 places yet defined NOWHERE in the store (grep -c = 0), so it is permanently `undefined`/falsy — this kills the ESC→pause-menu path (App.jsx:210) AND lets the legacy "CRAFTY RPG" click-to-play overlay (App.jsx:759, off-design slate-blue) render SIMULTANEOUSLY with the purple title menu on every mid-game ESC (the exact "flashes on ESC" defect). (2) KeyF is a triple-conflicting legacy path: keydown fires MELEE (Components:393) while held-F in the frame loop continuously CASTS spells (Components:1230) — contradicting the LMB-melee/RMB-cast verb router and the two overlays' own mutually-contradictory hints ("F Cast" vs "Left-Click Melee"). The keyMap anti-drift test gives FALSE confidence (it only string-matches `'Escape'`, never checks the pause behavior, and doesn't read App.jsx). Visuals lag SOTA: raw #fdbcb4 flesh-box hands, no movement accel/momentum, no coyote-time/jump-buffer, and a touch joystick whose visual nub never follows the thumb.

audits.summary: Combat is a real, layered system: 4 elemental spell projectiles with telegraph→flight→impact arcs, a GPU-instanced 1200-spark pool, melee cone + ribbon trail, a 3-phase boss, trauma screenshake, tiered hitstop, and element zones. BUT the operator's core complaint is CODE-TRUE: every spell projectile/trail/impact/telegraph is ONE shared geometry tinted by an element color, and the per-element silhouette mesh (the only true shape differentiator) is rendered as the DIMMEST occluded layer beneath dominant near-white additive core+glow spheres — so fire/ice/lightning/arcane read as the same white ball in different hues. The death-burst is genuinely broken: it passes a bogus 'death' spark type the GPU pool has no branch for, falling to a generic radial puff, and the additive glow shader (vColor * up to 5.0) washes any tint toward white. Plus dead/legacy code: SPELL_EFFECTS const (orphan), SpellLeftHandEffects (returns null), shield/heal mana costs for non-existent spells, the 'freeze' secondary is a silent no-op, and mob hits triple-stack two parallel ring-shockwave systems.
topPriorities: Fix per-element spell SHAPE legibility (spellVfx.jsx SpellProjectileCore): the silhouette mesh is renderOrder 0 + opacity 0.9 + occluded by depthTest:false near-white core/glow spheres that dominate. Make the SHAPE the hero (boost shape emissive/scale, shrink/dim the white core, give trail+impact+telegraph per-element GEOMETRY not just hue) — XS-S effort, high impact, directly the operator's stated grievance.

Fix the death-burst (mobHitFx.js deathBurst + SimplifiedNPCSystem.jsx:560 + GPUSparkSystem): it sends type='death' which has NO velocity branch in the GPU shader (falls to default radial) and the additive glow (vColor*(1.5+vLife*3.5)) blows the mob-color toward WHITE, so a kill reads as a generic white/green puff with no per-element death shape. Add a 'death' branch + cap glow gain or add a saturated colored-core pass.

PURGE dead code: SPELL_EFFECTS (GameSystems.jsx:148, orphan duplicate of spells.js secondary), SpellLeftHandEffects (playerRender.jsx:446 always returns null), shield:30/heal:40 in SPELL_MANA_COSTS (no SPELL_TYPES entry, castSpell early-returns) — XS effort, removes operator-flagged legacy-survives-cleanup pattern.

Resolve the iceball 'freeze' secondary no-op (EnhancedMagicSystem.jsx:346 case 'freeze': break;): slowPercent/freezeChance/stunDuration in SPELL_TYPES.iceball + lightning are defined but NEVER applied — either implement slow/freeze/stun or delete the dead config so the spell data matches lived behavior.

Consolidate the THREE overlapping impact-ring systems: spell SpellImpactPop ring (spellVfx.jsx) + legacy ImpactShockwave ring (combatVfx.jsx, mob hits) + the lava ring — mob hits render BOTH a GPU spark burst AND a flat ImpactShockwave ring; unify on one pooled ring grammar.
deadOrLegacyOrError: frontend/src/GameSystems.jsx:148 — SPELL_EFFECTS const is defined but NEVER imported or read anywhere (grep-confirmed single hit); a stale duplicate of the `secondary` blocks in game/spells.js. DELETE.
frontend/src/render/playerRender.jsx:446 — SpellLeftHandEffects({spellType}) unconditionally returns null (dead component); still mounted at playerRender.jsx:415. DELETE component + its mount.

frontend/src/GameSystems.jsx:144-145 — SPELL_MANA_COSTS.shield (30) and .heal (40) are dead config: no SPELL_TYPES entry exists for shield/heal, and castSpell does `if (!spell) return`, so they can never be charged/cast. PURGE.

frontend/src/EnhancedMagicSystem.jsx:346 — `case 'freeze': break;` is a silent no-op; SPELL_TYPES.iceball.secondary {slowPercent:70, freezeChance:0.2, duration:5} is never applied to mobs. Dead behavior vs. data.
frontend/src/game/spells.js:60 — lightning.secondary.stunDuration:1 is never consumed (chain applies damage only, no stun); arcane.secondary also unused fields. Dead tuning fields.

frontend/src/game/spellVisualProfiles.js / spells.js — DUPLICATED color systems: SPELL_TYPES (color/trailColor/particleColor/glowColor), ENERGY_PROFILE (coreColor/glowColor), SPARK_PROFILE, SPELL_COLORS, and combatVfx gradient switch each independently hardcode the 4 element palettes — 5 parallel sources of truth that drift (e.g. fireball glow is #FF6600 vs #FF7A1A vs #FF4500 across files).

frontend/src/SimplifiedNPCSystem.jsx:560 — death-burst calls triggerGPUSparks(..., 'death'); GPUSparkSystem has no 'death' velocity branch → silently falls through to the default radial profile, contradicting the deathBurst() per-mob-color finisher intent.

frontend/src/render/combatVfx.jsx ImpactShockwave + spellVfx.jsx SpellImpactPop — two parallel additive ground-ring shockwave implementations with near-identical math; ImpactShockwave is the older per-mob-hit ring that now double-renders alongside the GPU spark burst.

frontend/src/game/spells.js — trailLength (20-30) and particleCount (12-20) per spell are LEGACY fields from the old per-sphere trail/impact system (now replaced by the single stretch-billboard trail + GPU pool); trailLength is still read by the projectile trail ring-buffer but particleCount is dead.

frontend/src/render/spellVfx.jsx:8-15 — header documents a previously-shipped broken-main bug (`_trailDir is not defined` every frame after the iter-152 extraction); now fixed, but the verbose post-mortem comment is legacy noise.
frontend/src/EnhancedMagicSystem.jsx:454-472 — a large trailing block-comment after the component export describes the OLD flat-sphere projectile; documentation-only legacy, no code.

elements.recommendation: Keep the roster but prune dead fields (particleCount, iceball freeze sub-fields, lightning stunDuration) and collapse the 5 duplicate palette sources into one. Only 4 spells is THIN vs a 2026 action-RPG bar — consider a 5th utility/AoE spell post-cleanup.

elements.name: Spell projectile body (SpellProjectileCore — the per-element shape problem)
elements.state: spellVfx.jsx:90-204. THREE layers: (1) per-element silhouette mesh (sphere/crystal/bolt/swirl) at renderOrder 0, opacity 0.9, depthWrite off; (2) near-white additive inner core sphere (coreScale ~0.5-0.62) + a nested pure-white hotspot at renderOrder 2/3 with depthTest:false; (3) saturated outer glow shell. The white core+hotspot paint OVER everything with depthTest disabled and dominate; the colored shape is the dimmest, occluded layer.
elements.recommendation: Make the SHAPE the hero: raise the silhouette mesh emissiveIntensity + scale so it reads first; shrink the white hotspot and drop core opacity so the heart is a small spec, not a dominant ball; consider giving each element a genuinely different silhouette mass (jagged bolt vs round fireball vs angular crystal) at full brightness. This is the operator's #1 stated grievance — it is CODE-TRUE.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA stylized projectiles (Brawl Stars / worldofclaudecraft) read instantly by SHAPE at a glance — a fireball is a roiling sphere, lightning a forked bolt. Crafty's are all 'white ball + colored halo'. Gap = shape-invariance; the per-element geometry exists but is rendered as the weakest layer.

elements.recommendation: The trail is ONE shared cylinder shape tinted per element (operator-flagged 'trail is one shared shape tinted by color'). Differentiate: fire = soft turbulent taper, ice = sharp shard streak, lightning = thin jagged segments, arcane = ribbon. At minimum vary width/taper/opacity per element via the profile.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA trails are element-specific (smoke vs frost vs arc vs ribbon). Crafty's is a uniform cone. Gap = per-element trail geometry/behavior.

elements.recommendation: Same shape-invariance problem: the ring + white flash are identical across elements, only the hue changes. Add per-element impact GEOMETRY (fire = expanding fireball burst, ice = radial shard shatter, lightning = forked flash, arcane = imploding rune). Also it overlaps the legacy ImpactShockwave ring on mob hits — unify.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA impacts are bespoke per element. Crafty = one ring + one flash, tinted. Gap = impact shape variety + dedup of the two ring systems.

elements.recommendation: Tasteful but, again, ONE shared rune-circle tinted per element (the code comment admits the richer glyph vocabulary is a deferred Kevin taste-gate). For SOTA distinctness, give each element a different sigil/glyph. Currently flagged in KEVIN-REVIEW-BATCH, not finalized.

elements.name: GPU spark system (1200-spark instanced additive pool)
elements.state: world/GPUSparkSystem.jsx. ONE instancedMesh, custom vertex shader (physics: pos+vel*t+0.5*g*t², GPU billboarding), per-type velocity profiles (fireball ring / iceball tight / lightning fast / arcane swirl / physical), ring-buffer index, capture-deterministic via seeded RNG + pinned uTime. Fragment glow = vColor*(1.5+vLife*3.5), additive.
elements.recommendation: Core system is SOLID and SOTA-shaped (KEEP the architecture). Two issues: (a) glow gain up to 5.0× additive washes saturated colors toward WHITE (the operator's death-puff-reads-white finding) — cap the gain or add a saturated non-additive core; (b) no 'death' branch so death-burst sparks fall to default radial. Fix both.
elements.sotaGap: GPU-instanced sparks ARE the SOTA approach. Gap is purely the additive-white-wash + missing death profile, not the system.

audits.category: E — ENTITIES (mobs, NPCs, boss, AI, spawning, capture, loot)
audits.summary: Crafty's entity layer is a competent procedural-cube creature system but is far below a SOTA-June-2026 bar: every creature is a parametrically-resized box-stack (body+head+4 legs / 8 spider legs) with optional silhouette feature-boxes (horns/ribs/crest), red `#ff0000` eyes, and a floating plane health-bar. There are 10 mob types + 1 apex boss (Shadow Dragon at the fixed Blight Heart lair). There is NO populated hub, NO static NPCs/vendors/quest-givers, NO nametags — the lone "NPC" is the villager, a passive WANDERING mob with a 6-line random greeting and a trade Modal (proximity G-key). Two parallel companion systems coexist: the CURRENT soulbind/snare squad (X-key, talent-gated, miniplex `isAlly` archetype, real + tested) and the LEGACY pet system (T-key tame pigs/cows, extracted verbatim from the deleted AdvancedGameFeatures) which is redundant, has a broken non-boss attack-targeting path (reads `m.x`/`m.z` but mobEntities store `position[]`), and collides on the T-key. audits.summary: Crafty's HUD is FEATURE-RICH but cluttered and carries two confirmed legacy defects exactly matching Kevin's frustration. The dead `gameStarted` flag (NEVER set anywhere in the store) silently breaks the desktop pause-menu-on-ESC path (App.jsx:210) AND the relock-on-modal-close path (MenuSystem 3x), so on ESC the player instead sees a LEGACY off-design-system "CRAFTY RPG" slate/blue click-to-play overlay (App.jsx:734-805) that never got rebuilt to the bold-flat system — this is the "legacy menu overlay that flashes on ESC + dead gameStarted flag that survived cleanup" verbatim. The always-on CONTROLS cheat-sheet (top-right, w-52) plus the QuestTracker (top-left), Compass strip, ObjectiveTracker, day-phase dial, minimap, 3 stat pills (two reading identical 100/100), spell label, XP bar and four conditional Aspect meters together produce heavy "tech-demo" screen occupation vs a SOTA restrained HUD. The first-session onboarding (App.jsx:136, +8.6s second toast, 4s auto-dismiss, localStorage-once) gives no persistent spawn guidance for returning players — partly mitigated by ObjectiveTracker but the toast itself is a weak teaching surface. The Tooltip primitive is fully orphaned (exported, zero consumers). Classic-RPG completeness cues — combat log, ability cooldown sweeps, framed target/unit nameplate — are entirely ABSENT. Most individual panels are well-built on the locked bold-flat primitives.

audits.summary: The audio layer is broad and the synth voice-bank (synthVoices.js + aspectMotifs.js, ~40 voices) is genuinely high-quality, all-procedural, headroom-guarded, and unit-tested (14 audio tests green). Music is 3 distinct ElevenLabs tracks (day/night/boss) with a working crossfade. BUT the architecture has a load-bearing routing defect: the "SFX overhaul" master-bus limiter (masterBus.js) is wired ONLY into the muted procedural pad/arp and the rarely-used non-spatial fallbacks — the ENTIRE spatial SFX system (combat, footsteps, spells, all Aspect verbs, mob aggro, blocks — i.e. ~all real in-game sound) routes THREE.PositionalAudio → listener.gain → ctx.destination, BYPASSING the limiter. Consequently the in-game mix is UNLIMITED (the exact clip-on-sum problem the limiter was built to solve is unsolved for the sounds that fire), and the "Sound Effects" volume slider + "Mute All" toggle (which only drive the master-bus gain) are NON-FUNCTIONAL for spatial SFX. A store field `volume` read by the occlusion loop does not exist (always undefined). The muted procedural music (PROC_MUSIC_GAIN=0) is dead weight (~420 LOC of synthPad+arpeggiator scheduler) yet still SPAWNS an audible biome wind bed, which overlaps a SECOND always-on procedural wind in GameScene. Several voices/wrappers are dead.
topPriorities: Route ALL spatial SFX through the master-bus limiter (fix listener.gain → masterBus.input instead of ctx.destination in GameScene SpatialAudioController) — restores the limiter's purpose AND makes the sfxVolume slider + Mute All actually control in-game sound. Currently both are dead controls. XS-S code change, critical impact.

Decide the procedural-music fate: PROC_MUSIC_GAIN=0 makes ~420 LOC of synthPad+arpeggiator dead, but startSynthPad still spawns an AUDIBLE biome wind bed — either fully delete the pad/arp (keep only the wind bed on its own lifecycle) or restore it. Right now it is the largest dead-but-side-effecting block in the audio layer.

Consolidate the TWO redundant wind beds (GameScene always-on procedural wind + SoundManager biome wind bed) into one biome-aware bed routed through the bus; today they run simultaneously and double the wind energy.

Purge dead voices/wrappers: `magic` voice (legacy, superseded by magicCast, 0 callers), `magicCharge` voice + playMagicCharge (0 callers), playJump/playMagic/playTone wrappers (0 callers; jump fires via direct spatial call).

Add a test that asserts spatial SFX route through the limiter (current masterBus.test only checks the bus in isolation — it green-passed while the real game audio bypasses the bus entirely; this is the 'gate measured presence not lived result' trap).
deadOrLegacyOrError: frontend/src/SoundManager.jsx:13 PROC_MUSIC_GAIN=0 — mutes the entire procedural synthPad + arpeggiator (~lines 56-432), leaving ~420 LOC dead-but-executing (still creates oscillators/intervals/wind bed each session)

frontend/src/SoundManager.jsx:367-389 makeMagicSound + VOICES.magic (synthVoices.js:717) — legacy 'magic' voice, 0 callers (superseded by magicCast); playMagic wrapper (SoundManager:612) also 0 callers
frontend/src/synthVoices.js:461-480 makeMagicChargeSound + VOICES.magicCharge — 0 trigger sites; only the playMagicCharge wrapper (SoundManager:633) references it and that wrapper has 0 callers
frontend/src/SoundManager.jsx:609 playJump wrapper — 0 callers (jump SFX fires via direct playSpatialSound('jump',...) at Components.jsx:1001)
frontend/src/SoundManager.jsx:542-564 playTone — 0 callers (only exposed in context value, never invoked)

frontend/src/GameScene.jsx:379 useGameStore.getState().volume — store field 'volume' does NOT exist (store has sfxVolume/musicVolume, not volume); always undefined, so freshVolume silently falls back to the SoundProvider useState volume (0.5, never changed by any UI)

frontend/src/SoundManager.jsx:28-31,566-572 setSoundEnabled/setMusicEnabled/setVolume — exposed in context but wired to NO UI control (orphaned API; the only audio UI is the sfxVolume/musicVolume/masterMuted store sliders which bypass these)
frontend/src/GameScene.jsx:315-349 second procedural wind generator — duplicates the SoundManager biome wind bed (both audible simultaneously); routes to ctx.destination, bypassing the bus

elements.name: masterBus.js — single limiter (gain → DynamicsCompressor → destination)
elements.state: Pure factory, unit-tested (3 tests). Built S3 'SFX overhaul' to stop peaks summing/clipping. Limiter params: threshold -3dB, knee 0, ratio 20, attack 3ms, release 250ms.
elements.sotaGap: The node itself is fine, but its INTEGRATION is broken: only the muted pad/arp + non-spatial playSound/playTone fallbacks route through getMasterBus(). The spatial SFX system (the real game audio) bypasses it (see GameScene element). A SOTA mix has ONE limiter at the master before destination that ALL voices pass through.
elements.recommendation: Keep the node. Fix the wiring: make the THREE.AudioListener's gain feed masterBus.input instead of ctx.destination, so spatial SFX are limited too.

elements.name: SpatialAudioController — THREE.PositionalAudio bridge + occlusion + cavern reverb (GameScene)
elements.state: Carries virtually ALL in-game SFX (combat hit/attack/swing, footsteps, jump, blocks, all Aspect verbs grab/hurl/slam/anvil/bind/roar, mob aggroGrowl, element zone ignite/freeze/zap/rune, magicCast/Hit/Explosion, motifs). Per-sound lowpass occlusion via Rapier raycast (1/2/3+ intersections → 3500/1200/350Hz + gain duck), depth-based muffle + cavern delay-feedback reverb. ±7% pitch jitter. Routes listener.gain → filter → ctx.destination.
elements.sotaGap: Functionally rich (occlusion + reverb is above-bar for a web voxel game), but it BYPASSES the master-bus limiter → the in-game mix can clip on simultaneous hits (the very problem the limiter was added for), and it reads a nonexistent store field `volume` (always undefined → fixed 0.5). SOTA: route the listener through the master bus; drive volume from the real sfxVolume store field.
elements.recommendation: Reroute listener.gain → masterBus.input → limiter → destination. Replace getState().volume with the actual store SFX volume. Keep the occlusion/reverb math.

elements.name: SFX 'Sound Effects' volume slider + 'Mute All' toggle (settings UI)
elements.state: GamePanels.jsx sliders write sfxVolume/musicVolume/masterMuted to the store; SoundManager effect applies audioGain(sfxVolume,masterMuted) to the master-bus INPUT gain. UI labels claim Mute All 'silences BOTH the SFX bus AND the music'.
elements.sotaGap: Because spatial SFX bypass the master bus, the SFX slider and Mute All have NO effect on the real in-game SFX (combat/footsteps/spells/verbs/mobs). They only affect the few non-spatial sounds (UI foley, heartbeat, siegeHorn, dawnChime, fanfare, victory, levelUp, defeat). A user lowering 'Sound Effects' or hitting 'Mute All' still hears full-volume combat. This is a visible, shippable-to-prod control that lies about what it does.
elements.recommendation: Resolved by routing spatial SFX through the bus (above). Then the slider/mute become correct. Verify with a runtime test.

elements.sotaGap: Good idea (per-biome wind character) but coupled to the dead synthPad lifecycle (only starts if startSynthPad runs, i.e. music enabled) and DUPLICATES the always-on procedural wind in GameScene → two wind layers stack. SOTA: one biome-aware wind bed with its own lifecycle, independent of music on/off.
elements.recommendation: Extract to its own small component/ref decoupled from the synthPad; delete the GameScene procedural wind; keep biomeAmbience.js (clean pure map).

elements.name: GameScene procedural wind (SpatialAudioController, lines 315-376)
elements.state: A separate always-on (when soundEnabled) looping noise → lowpass(500Hz→height/day-modulated) → gain 0.03..0.05*volume → ctx.destination. Frequency/gain driven by camera height + isDay in useFrame.
elements.sotaGap: Redundant with the biome wind bed; bypasses the bus; reads the same fixed-0.5 volume. Two independent wind generators is a content defect (doubled wind, no biome character here).
elements.recommendation: DELETE; fold its height/day modulation into the single biome wind bed if that nuance is wanted.

elements.name: synthVoices.js voice bank (makeTone/Noise + ~35 named voices)
elements.state: All-synth pure (ctx)=>AudioBuffer factories: combat (attack/hit/defeat/swing), blocks (blockPlace/Break), footstep/jump/pickup/craft, verbs (roar/aggroGrowl/grab/hurl/slam/anvilHit/bind), elements (ignite/freeze/zap/rune), magic (magicCast/Hit/Explosion/Charge + legacy magic), transitions (siegeHorn/dawnChime), rewards (fanfare/victory/levelUp), UI (uiOpen/uiClose), heartbeat. Headroom-guarded (slam/siegeHorn/dawnChime/fanfare/victory normalize if peak>0.98). 6 characterization tests green.
elements.sotaGap: Genuinely strong for a web game — distinct, designed, clip-safe procedural voices with good musical detail (FM anvil, lydian elemancer motif, lub-dub heartbeat). Minor: makeTone's hard square wave + uniform exp(-t*5) envelope on block/jump/pickup/craft is the most generic-sounding subset.
elements.recommendation: KEEP. Optional polish: richer envelopes on the makeTone-based block/jump/pickup voices. Purge the dead `magic` + `magicCharge` voices.

elements.name: aspectMotifs.js — per-Aspect stinger arpeggios
elements.state: makeArp + 4 motifs (Wildheart A2-C3-E3 saw; Voidhand E3-B2-E2 square; Soulbind G3-B3-D4-G4 tri; Elemancer C4-E4-F#4-B4 lydian sine+shimmer). Fired spatially at signature moments (transform/slam/fuse/first-zone). Headroom-normalized.
elements.sotaGap: Above-bar identity design (each Aspect has a melodic signature). No gap.
elements.recommendation: KEEP as-is.

elements.name: MusicPlayer.jsx — ElevenLabs day/night/boss tracks + crossfade
elements.state: Loads /music/{day,night,boss}.mp3 (3 distinct files confirmed by md5), picks by context (boss>night>day), 60ms-tick volume crossfade toward VOL(0.4)*musicVolume, master-mute aware, capture-safe, musicEnabled-gated. Mounted in HUD.
elements.sotaGap: Clean. Minor: autoplay may defer until gesture (handled). The musicVolume slider works here (unlike the SFX slider). One nit: tracks created in a mount effect with no error fallback if an mp3 404s (would silently no-op).
elements.recommendation: KEEP. Optional: a missing-asset guard/log.

elements.name: DayNightAudio.jsx — siege horn / dawn chime transition stings
elements.state: Edge-watcher on isDay; day→night fires playSiegeHorn (non-spatial), night→day fires playDawnChime. No sound on mount. Mounted in HUD. Capture-safe.
elements.sotaGap: Correct pattern. Non-spatial → DOES respond to the SFX slider/mute (one of the few that do).
elements.recommendation: KEEP.

elements.name: HeartbeatAudio.jsx — low-health lub-dub
elements.state: Edge-watcher on quantized lowHealthIntensity bucket (0..4); plays heartbeat on a setInterval whose period quickens via heartbeatPeriod as HP→0. Non-spatial. Mounted in HUD.
elements.sotaGap: Solid survival cue. Non-spatial → responds to SFX slider/mute.
elements.recommendation: KEEP.

elements.name: UISounds.jsx — panel open/close foley
elements.state: Edge-watcher on (showInventory||showCrafting||showMagic||showBuildingTools); plays uiOpen/uiClose. Non-spatial. Mounted in HUD.
elements.sotaGap: Fine. Only watches 4 panels — other overlays (trading, talent, spell-upgrade, settings, quests) open silently. Minor completeness gap.
elements.recommendation: KEEP; optionally extend the watched-panel set for uniform foley.

elements.name: audioSettings.js (audioGain) + store sfxVolume/musicVolume/masterMuted
elements.state: Pure audioGain(volume,muted) clamp, 3 tests green. Store has sfxVolume/musicVolume/masterMuted + setters (lines 295-304). musicVolume + masterMuted correctly affect MusicPlayer; sfxVolume + masterMuted only affect the master-bus gain.
elements.sotaGap: audioGain is clean. The store has NO `volume` field, yet GameScene's occlusion loop reads getState().volume — a latent bug/dead read. The sfxVolume control's effect is severed from spatial SFX (see slider element).
elements.recommendation: KEEP audioGain. Add/rename so spatial SFX read the real sfxVolume; remove the phantom getState().volume read.

elements.name: resumeAudio + AudioContext lifecycle
elements.state: Single AudioContext created on SoundProvider mount; resumeAudio() (idempotent, suspended-only) called on pointer-lock entry in App.jsx independent of music so SFX work with music off. THREE.AudioContext.setContext unifies the ctx with the THREE listener.
elements.sotaGap: Correct handling of the browser autoplay-suspend gotcha + ctx unification. No gap.
elements.recommendation: KEEP.

elements.name: Non-spatial playSound/playTone fallback path
elements.state: playSound creates a bufferSource → gain(volume) → getMasterBus()||destination. Used by heartbeat, siegeHorn, dawnChime, fanfare, victory, levelUp, defeat (when no pos), uiOpen/Close, and as the no-position fallback for spatial wrappers. This path DOES go through the limiter.
elements.sotaGap: Asymmetry: this fallback path is limited + slider-controlled, but the primary spatial path is not → inconsistent loudness/control between the two. Resolving the spatial routing unifies them.
elements.recommendation: KEEP; the fix to spatial routing removes the asymmetry.

elements.name: Voice triggering coverage (which voices actually fire)
elements.state: Live: footstep, jump, blockPlace, blockBreak, pickup, craft, attack, swing, hit, defeat, magicCast, magicHit, magicExplosion, levelUp, fanfare, victory, heartbeat, siegeHorn, dawnChime, uiOpen, uiClose, roar, aggroGrowl, grab, hurl, slam, anvilHit, bind, ignite, freeze, zap, rune, all 4 motifs. UNFIRED: magic (legacy), magicCharge.
elements.sotaGap: 33/35 voices fire — strong coverage. Only `magic` (superseded by magicCast) and `magicCharge` (no charge-up mechanic wired) are unfired.
elements.recommendation: Delete `magic` + `magicCharge` voices + their wrappers; OR wire magicCharge to a spell channel/charge mechanic if one is planned.

audits.summary: The progression spine is broad and mostly wired end-to-end: XP/level (progression.js, derive-on-read), a 12-quest authored ladder + endless bounty fallback, 12 achievements, 7-mob loot tables with distance-tier rarity scaling, ~21-recipe crafting, an item registry of 39 ids, equipment with stat-folding, a 4-Aspect talent tree, four genuinely-wired Aspect resource economies (Ferocity/Kinetic/Soul/Resonance with real spend constants), a coin/crystal/scroll merchant, the Pilgrimage→Blight-Heart→Shadow-Dragon objective spine, and a VictoryOverlay win-state. 1458/1459 unit tests pass (the 1 failure is a bloom/visual gate, not Category H). BUT the surface hides a cluster of dead/no-op/incoherent mechanics that survived prior cleanups: Mana Potion restores ZERO mana (window.addMana never defined — live functional bug); spell-upgrade manaCost escalation and xpCost are both never applied (upgrades are free + cost-free, UI lies); chest Damage/Shield Scrolls + starting scrolls are a dead currency (no buff system consumes them); Apple is a phantom consumable; the boss climax rewards two unregistered junk items; and the legacy pet-tame system is a redundant, lying duplicate of the SOULBIND squad. Depth/balance vs a SOTA-June-2026 action-RPG is shallow: 3-step linear spell tiers, no affixes/sockets/set-bonuses, a 3-tier flat loot table, and a self-trivializing starting inventory that pre-stocks every sword + most armor.
topPriorities: Fix the Mana Potion no-op: window.addMana is never defined (GamePanels.jsx:206) — consuming a Mana Potion deletes the item and restores 0 mana. Route to useGameStore.getState().setMana / a real restoreMana action. XS effort, high impact (a core consumable is silently broken in prod).

Resolve the spell-upgrade cost incoherence: xpCost (spellUpgrades.js) is never spent (only proxied into a player-level gate) and the escalating manaCost (L2/L3) is never charged at cast (EnhancedMagicSystem uses static SPELL_MANA_COSTS). Either wire the costs or delete the dead fields + fix the UI that shows them. S effort, high impact (free strictly-better upgrades = balance hole + UI-vs-reality lie).

Purge dead economy content: chest Damage/Shield Scrolls (buff_damage/buff_defense effects never applied), the 'scrolls' currency (starting 4 + Crystals→Scroll trade, never consumed), and the phantom 'Apple' consumable (referenced in 3 sites, never obtainable). S effort, med impact.

Fix the boss climax reward: bossSystem.js grants 'Crown of the Dragon King' + 'Dragon Scale' which are NOT in the ITEMS registry (no icon, default 'common' rarity) and passes a 3rd 'Legendary'/'Epic' arg that addToInventory silently ignores. The final win reward is unidentifiable common junk. S effort, med-high impact (it's the payoff of the whole run).

De-duplicate companions: the legacy petSystem (T-key, tames pigs/cows, message lies about needing wheat/apples that don't exist) overlaps the newer SOULBIND snare/squad/hybrid system (X-key, real Soul economy). Decide one canonical path; the pet system's gate is incoherent. M effort, med impact.
deadOrLegacyOrError: frontend/src/ui/GamePanels.jsx:206 — Mana Potion consume calls window.addMana(40), but window.addMana is NEVER defined anywhere in src. Live no-op bug: potion is removed, 0 mana restored.

frontend/src/world/spellUpgrades.js:14-44 — xpCost field on every spell-level row is NEVER spent; only used (spellUpgrades.js:88, SpellUpgradePanel.jsx:19) to derive a player-level gate. Misleading dead data masquerading as a cost.

frontend/src/world/spellUpgrades.js:13-44 — leveled manaCost (L2/L3: 18/22, 15/19, 30/35, 22/28) is never charged at cast; EnhancedMagicSystem.jsx:157 always uses static SPELL_MANA_COSTS. Shown as 'Cost' in Spell Mastery UI (SpellUpgradePanel.jsx:201) → UI-vs-reality divergence.

frontend/src/data/lootTables.js:67-68 — Damage Scroll (buff_damage) + Shield Scroll (buff_defense) drop from chests but the effects are NEVER applied (no buff/timed-effect system consumes effect/value/duration). Dead chest loot; openChest only spawns them as inert pickups.
frontend/src/store/useGameStore.jsx:567 — starting magic.scrolls:4 + TradingInterface.jsx:85 'Crystals to Scroll' trade produce 'scrolls', but scrolls are NEVER consumed anywhere. Dead currency.

frontend/src/ui/GamePanels.jsx:92,188,198 — 'Apple' is a consumable branch (feed 20 / heal 5) but Apple is NEVER obtainable (absent from ITEMS, all loot tables, recipes, dawn rewards, trades). Dead consumable.

frontend/src/world/bossSystem.js:86-87 — boss grants 'Crown of the Dragon King' + 'Dragon Scale', neither in ITEMS registry (no icon, falls to 'common' rarity); the 3rd arg ('Legendary'/'Epic') is ignored by addToInventory (useGameStore.jsx:573, arity 2).

frontend/src/MenuSystem.jsx:198,220,230 + frontend/src/App.jsx:203,210 — gameState.gameStarted is read in 5 guards but NEVER defined/set in the store (no gameStarted key, no setGameStarted). Permanently falsy → dead pointer-lock-reacquire + pre-game-suppression paths. The exact dead-flag class the mandate flagged.

frontend/src/world/petSystem.js:21-24 — tame failure message 'Only pigs and cows can be tamed with wheat/apples!' lies: there is NO wheat/apple held-item gate (InputManager.jsx:197-213 tames on T-key + proximity to any passive mob). Misleading/legacy; overlaps the SOULBIND squad system.

frontend/src/data/items.js:33-34 — ITEMS ids 'sword' and 'pickaxe' have lowercase display names ('sword','pickaxe') unlike every other item's Title Case; inconsistent + collides conceptually with the inventory.tools bucket (pickaxe/sword) — duplicate identity surface.

elements.name: XP & Leveling (progression.js + grantXP)
elements.state: xpForLevel = floor(100*1.5^(level-1)); grantXP (useGameStore.jsx:328) loops multi-level on a single grant, awards +5 attribute points & +1 talent point per level, heals to full on level-up, derives maxHealth/maxMana on read (never bakes). Clean, unit-tested, save/respec-safe.
elements.recommendation: Keep the math core. SOTA action-RPGs add a soft level cap, prestige/paragon, and per-source XP scaling vs enemy level (anti-grind). Optional XL enhancement, not a defect.
elements.sotaGap: No level cap / paragon / diminishing returns; 1.5^n curve grows unbounded (level 20 needs ~222k XP). Functional but flat.

elements.name: Loot tables (per-mob drops)
elements.state: LOOT_TABLES (lootTables.js) covers all 12 hostile mob keys (pig/cow/zombie/skeleton/spider + skitterling/duskhound/moss_brute/emberhusk variety pass); each row {item,chance,xp}; a loot-coverage gate asserts every hostile drops something & every item is a valid registry name. Distance zoneTier boosts non-common chances (lootTier.js, +25%/tier cap +100%).
elements.recommendation: Solid for a voxel-RPG. Keep. The tier-rarity scaling is a nice risk/reward touch.
elements.sotaGap: Flat 3-rarity model; no item-level scaling, no smart-loot (class weighting), no quantity rolls (always qty 1), no rarity upgrade on far-tier (only chance). SOTA ARPGs roll item-level + affixes per drop.

elements.name: Loot pickup → inventory path
elements.state: spawnLootDrop (SimplifiedNPCSystem.jsx:32) adds an ECS loot entity; stepLootDrop magnet-collects (range 7); on collect → addToInventory + grantXP + rarity-tinted pop + pickup SFX + notification. Capture-deterministic. Fully wired.
elements.recommendation: Keep — this is the healthiest part of the loop.
elements.sotaGap: None material.

elements.name: Item registry (ITEMS, 39 ids)
elements.state: items.js: 39 stable ids → {name, icon, rarity}; NAME_TO_ID/NAME_TO_RARITY maps; getItemRarity/getItemIcon/getItemName with emoji-strip + substring fallback. The single SoT for icon+rarity. Well-structured.
elements.recommendation: Fix the two lowercase-name items ('sword','pickaxe', items.js:33-34) to Title Case for consistency. Add the boss-reward items (Crown of the Dragon King, Dragon Scale) or stop granting them. Consider folding the parallel inventory.tools bucket (pickaxe/shovel/axe/sword) into the registry to kill the dual identity surface.
elements.sotaGap: No item metadata depth: no stackSize, no sell value, no flavor text, no item-level, no affix slots. It's a name/icon/rarity lookup, not an item model.

elements.name: Item icons
elements.state: icon field maps to gameIcons primitive names (sword/shield/helmet/potion/etc.). Unknown items (e.g. boss junk) return null → caller renders a BLOCK_TYPES color swatch (no broken-emoji). Zero-emoji gate passes.
elements.recommendation: Keep. Just ensure every grantable item has a registry entry so none fall to the null-icon swatch path (boss items currently do).
elements.sotaGap: None material; the 2-tone game-icons set is on-brand.

elements.name: Crafting recipes (~21)
elements.state: recipes.js: 21 recipes — weapons (stone/iron/diamond sword), shields, full armor sets (leather/iron/diamond helm/chest/boots), Golden Crown, tools (pickaxe/bow→arrows/torch/glass/planks/magic crystal), cooking (raw→cooked over coal), 1 alchemy (Health Potion). Pattern-matched against a normalized 3x3 grid. Pure + unit-testable.
elements.recommendation: Coherent and the cook/alchemy loops close kill→raw→cook→eat. Add depth: more alchemy (Mana Potion recipe — currently uncraftable, only from chest/trade), tiered tools that gate mining, and recipe-discovery/unlock gating instead of all-visible. Note: Mana Potion has no recipe despite Health Potion having one — asymmetric.
elements.sotaGap: No tech-tree gating (all recipes always available), no quality/upgrade crafting, no enchanting/reforging, no material tiers beyond the vanilla stone/iron/diamond ladder. Shallow vs SOTA crafting ARPGs.

elements.name: Trading / coin economy (merchant)
elements.state: TradingInterface.jsx: 8 trades — coins→potions, blocks→crystals (stone/coal/iron/gold), crystals→scroll/wand. coins earned only from dawn survival (10*night); spendCoins is the sole coin sink (clamped ≥0). Block→crystal→wand chain works; the M5 lost-buy bug (items landing in unrendered 'magic') was fixed → routes to blocks.
elements.recommendation: The coin loop is thin (coins ONLY from surviving nights; only sinks are 2 potions). 'Crystals to Scroll' produces dead scrolls (no consumer) and 'Crystals to Wand' produces a wand that is never consumed/used in combat (cast uses mana, not wand). Remove dead trades or give wand/scroll a real effect. No merchant NPC spawn coherence checked here, but the panel + selectedVillager wiring exists.
elements.sotaGap: No dynamic pricing, no sell-back (one-way trades), no reputation/restock, no gold-as-universal-currency (coins are a survival-only token). SOTA economies have buy+sell+vendor-trash sinks.

elements.name: Aspects (4 trees) + Aspect resource economies
elements.state: talentTree.js: 4 Aspect trees (Voidhand/Wildheart/Soulbind/Elemancer), each with stat nodes (+str/agi/int/armor per rank, folded via foldTalentEffects) + effect-less SIGNATURE unlock nodes. Four real banked economies with genuine spend constants: Ferocity (kills→roar), Kinetic (GRAB_COST=25), Soul (SNARE_COST=35, FUSE_COST=50), Resonance (build→imbue, 30). Dawn bleeds all banks. aspectGuide.js documents each loop; all verified wired.
elements.recommendation: This is the strongest, most coherent progression pillar — keep. The 4 banked meters + signature verbs are genuinely SOTA-flavored (distinct per-Aspect resource loops). Minor: stat nodes are generic (+N to an attribute); signature verbs carry the identity.
elements.sotaGap: Stat nodes are flat (+3 str/rank); no branching synergies, no capstones beyond the single signature unlock, no respec UI (refundUnknownTalents only runs on save-migration). SOTA trees have cross-branch synergies + keystones.

elements.name: Talents (spend path + limits + migration)
elements.state: spendTalentPoint (useGameStore.jsx:472) enforces prereq + per-node limit, recomputes maxStats, fires a just-in-time aspect hint on first unlock. TALENT_LIMITS derived from trees. refundUnknownTalents migrates stale ids on load. Clean.
elements.recommendation: Keep. Add a respec/reset-talents action (currently the only refund path is the load-time stale-id migration; a player cannot voluntarily respec).
elements.sotaGap: No player-facing respec; no talent-point refund/relocation. Minor.

elements.name: Spell-upgrade system (#51 revived)
elements.state: spellUpgrades.js: 4 spells × 3 levels with escalating damage + manaCost + xpCost. useSpellUpgrades hook hydrates from save, pushes getSpellStats/upgradeSpell to store. Cast path (EnhancedMagicSystem.jsx:183 via resolveCastBaseDamage) reads LEVELED damage — so upgrades DO affect damage. BUT: xpCost is never spent (only proxies a player-level gate), and the leveled manaCost is never charged (cast uses static SPELL_MANA_COSTS). Spell Mastery UI shows the leveled 'Cost' that is never actually paid.
elements.recommendation: Two coupled defects: (1) upgrades are FREE (no resource spent — only a player-level gate), and (2) leveling raises damage but NOT the mana charged, so each level is strictly better at zero cost. Either spend xpCost (real XP/talent sink) and charge the leveled manaCost at cast, OR delete xpCost + the manaCost escalation and stop showing them. Current state is a balance hole + a UI lie.
elements.sotaGap: 3 linear tiers, no branching spell mods/runes/glyphs, no opportunity cost. SOTA spell systems have modifier trees + tradeoffs (more dmg = more cost/cast-time).

elements.name: Quests (12 authored + endless bounty)
elements.state: QUEST_LIST: 12 quests across 3 tiers (kill/kill_type/block_place/block_break/spell_cast/chest_open/reach_shrine/survive_nights). makeRepeatableQuest generates scaling Bounty_N once authored list exhausts (goal feed never dries). Active set capped at 3; claim grants XP + pulls next. updateQuestProgress handles kill→kill_type aggregation. Save/reseed via questState mirror.
elements.recommendation: Coherent, well-wired, endless-tail handled. Keep. Enhancement: quests reward ONLY XP — add item/coin/recipe rewards for variety. The Pilgrimage quest (reach_shrine) is the one objective-spine tie-in.
elements.sotaGap: XP-only rewards; no quest chains/branching, no choice-rewards, no faction/story quests. The bounty fallback is repetitive ('Defeat N mobs' forever). Functional but shallow.

elements.name: Quest rewards
elements.state: claimQuest grants quest.xpReward via GameMethods.grantXP + a notification. That is the ENTIRE reward — no items, no coins, no unlocks.
elements.recommendation: Diversify rewards (gear, coins, recipe unlocks, talent points) — XP-only makes all 12 quests feel identical in payoff. XS-to-S per reward-type.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA quest rewards are multi-currency + gated unlocks + cosmetic. Here it's a flat XP number.

elements.name: Achievements (12)
elements.state: ACHIEVEMENTS: 12 (kills/spells/chests/level/deaths/blocks milestones), auto-unlock on stat thresholds via checkAchievements, fanfare SFX, AchievementsPanel UI with stat summary. Persisted via questState. Wired.
elements.recommendation: Keep. Achievements give NO reward (pure cosmetic unlock) — that's acceptable for achievements, but consider tying a few to talent points or cosmetics for stickiness.
elements.sotaGap: No achievement rewards, no meta-progression, no rare/hidden achievements. Standard-tier.

elements.name: Treasure chests + chest loot
elements.state: useTreasureChests: periodic spawn (every 30s, max 5, 20-60 blocks from player) + initial chest + guaranteed shrine chests (exempt from cap, richer on far tiers). openChest rolls CHEST_LOOT (7 rows), guarantees ≥1 item, shrine chests add tier-scaled rolls from the rarer half. Capture-suppressed. G-key open. Wired.
elements.recommendation: Mechanically solid. But the loot itself is degraded: Damage/Shield Scrolls drop with effects that are never applied (dead), and XP-token items (Diamond/Golden Crown/Star Fragment as effect:'xp') overlap with their equippable/material identities (see item-identity-conflict element). Clean up the chest loot table to only contain items with live effects.
elements.sotaGap: Random-angle spawn (not placed in world structures); no locked/rare chests, no key economy, no mimic/trap variety. The spawn-near-player model is gamey vs world-placed loot.

elements.name: Coin/currency economy (coins · crystals · scrolls · wand)
elements.state: coins: dawn-survival only, spent on 2 potions. crystals: from gold→crystal trades + Magic Crystal recipe, spent on scrolls/wand. scrolls: starting 4 + crystal trade, NEVER consumed. wand: starting 1 + crystal trade, NEVER consumed in combat (cast uses mana). Three of four 'currencies' are partial dead-ends.
elements.recommendation: Coins + crystals have real sinks; scrolls and wand do not. Either give scrolls/wand a live effect (e.g. scroll = consumable buff, wand = required spell focus / mana-efficiency) or remove them from inventory + trades. Currently they are inventory clutter that the UI lets you 'buy' into a void.
elements.sotaGap: Fragmented non-fungible micro-currencies with dead sinks. SOTA: one primary currency + a few premium/crafting mats, all with sinks.

elements.name: Consumables (potions / food / XP tokens)
elements.state: handleConsume (GamePanels.jsx:191): Health Potion→heal 30 (works); Cooked→feed 40+heal 10 (works); Apple→feed 20+heal 5 (Apple unobtainable=dead); Raw→feed 15; Rotten Flesh→feed 10; Mana Potion→window.addMana(40) (BROKEN, addMana undefined); Diamond→+50 XP; Golden Crown→+200 XP; Star Fragment→+100 XP.
elements.recommendation: Fix Mana Potion (window.addMana undefined → 0 mana restored, item still consumed). Remove the dead Apple branch. Reconcile the XP-token consumes (Diamond/Golden Crown/Star Fragment) with their other identities (Diamond is also a craft material; Golden Crown is also a craftable equippable helmet) — same item, two contradictory consume paths.
elements.sotaGap: Hunger system exists (consumeHunger→starvation dmg) but food sustain is thin; no buff-food, no cooldowns on potions, no potion stacking rules.

elements.name: Item identity conflicts (Diamond / Golden Crown / Star Fragment)
elements.state: Diamond: craft material (recipes), chest XP-loot (effect:xp 50), AND consumable (+50 XP) — and legendary equippable? No. Golden Crown: equippable helmet (EQUIPMENT_STATS, craftable from gold) AND chest XP-loot (+200 XP consumable). Same name resolves to different behavior by code path (equip vs consume). Star Fragment: legendary, only a +100 XP token, no other role.
elements.recommendation: One item name must have ONE identity. Split: e.g. a distinct 'Dragon Egg'/'XP Tome' for XP tokens, keep Golden Crown as helmet-only, keep Diamond as material-only. Today consuming a Golden Crown for 200 XP destroys what is also your craftable epic helmet — a player trap.
elements.sotaGap: Overloaded item identities are an anti-pattern; SOTA keeps material/consumable/equipment as disjoint item classes.

elements.name: Equipment system + starting inventory
elements.state: EQUIPMENT_STATS (useGameStore.jsx:16) maps 18 gear names → str/agi/int/armor; equipItem/unequipItem swap inventory↔slot, recompute maxStats; GearInspector shows stat-diff vs equipped. BUT starting inventory (useGameStore.jsx:556) pre-stocks ALL 3 swords + 2 shields + Golden Crown + Iron Helmet + 2 chestplates + 2 boots + 5 Health + 5 Mana potions.
elements.recommendation: The starting inventory trivializes the entire loot+craft+economy loop — you begin with the Diamond Sword (best weapon) and near-full armor, so there is no progression incentive to farm/craft gear. Strip the starter kit to a basic tool + a few blocks. This single change would give the whole Category H loop a purpose.
elements.sotaGap: Day-one BiS gear = zero gear progression. SOTA ARPGs gate power behind the loot/craft loop; this hands it all over at spawn (likely a dev/test convenience that shipped).

elements.name: Pilgrimage → Blight-Heart objective spine + win-state
elements.state: Pilgrim quest (reach_shrine) → shrineReached flag flips HUD objective cue → Blight Heart at fixed far-frontier coord (blightHeart.js, ~1280 blocks NE, deep tier 4) → Shadow Dragon awakens when player (level≥5) reaches the lair (bossSystem.js, 3-phase boss) → on defeat: +600 XP, junk items, VictoryOverlay (GameSystems.jsx:241) with 'Keep exploring' endless handoff + victory sting. Coherent foreshadowed arc.
elements.recommendation: The spine is well-architected (learnable fixed destination, compass marker, 3-phase fight, real win-screen). Two gaps: (1) boss reward items are unregistered junk (see dead-code), (2) the only win-gate is level≥5 + travel — no escalating mid-journey objectives between shrine and lair. Fix the reward; consider mini-bosses/gates along the pilgrimage.
elements.sotaGap: Single linear objective with one boss; no act structure, no optional bosses, no NG+. The win-state is a dismissable overlay → endless mode (acceptable for a sandbox-RPG).

elements.name: Pets (legacy tame) vs SOULBIND squad (redundancy)
elements.state: petSystem.js: T-key tames pigs/cows on proximity (max 3), cycles follow/stay/attack orders, PetIndicator + PetEntities render. Message claims 'wheat/apples' needed — NO such gate exists. elements.recommendation: Either DELETE, or repurpose it as the renderer the phantom showMagic flag is missing (see next item). Given EnhancedMagicSystem is the live spell engine, this static list panel is likely just delete.

elements.name: Phantom showMagic panel surface (M-key opens nothing)
elements.state: showMagic is a fully-wired store flag (useGameStore.jsx:593-594) toggled by M (InputManager.jsx:120 'the advertised M key now opens Magic'), the touch tray (touchTray.js:13), the HUD Wand2 button (GameHud.jsx:74), and it gates UI sounds (UISounds.jsx:11) + touch panel state. So pressing M plays an open-sound, suppresses the title menu, and shows an empty screen.

The InputManager comment calls M 'an unhandled HUD lie' it was meant to fix — it's still a lie, just relocated.

elements.name: Prod combat console.log spam (i-frames + per-hit)
elements.state: useGameStore.jsx:705 console.log('[i-frames] Damage ignored (source:...)') fires on every i-frame-blocked hit; :720 console.log('[hit] Player hit by ${source}: raw damage...mitigated...Armor...DR...') fires on EVERY successful player hit. Both unguarded — they run in the deployed build, spamming the console during combat.
