elements.name: Other unguarded prod console logs
elements.state: GameScene.jsx:789 console.log('WebGL context successfully restored...') runs in prod. Components.jsx:823 console.warn('[DEBUG] Player fell into void!...') and :868 console.error('[DEBUG] Physics corrupted!...') are defensive recovery paths but [DEBUG]-tagged and unconditional. index.jsx:31-37 monkeypatches console.log/warn/error to tee into an in-app log buffer (intentional, but means every leftover log is doubly visible).
elements.recommendation: Drop the WebGL-restored log or make it DEV-only; keep the void/physics warns (genuine error telemetry) but drop the [DEBUG] prefix or route them through a proper logger.

elements.name: Half-wired backend dependency (boot 404 / CONN_REFUSED)
elements.state: AuthContext.jsx:19 and WorldManager.jsx:31 use VITE_BACKEND_URL || 'http://localhost:8001'. On mount AuthContext GETs /api/auth/me; if VITE_BACKEND_URL is unset (local capture, and likely the Vercel deploy) this fires net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED / 404 on EVERY load (confirmed in capture at @boot). Catch handlers fall back to LocalStorage so it's non-fatal, but it's console noise + a silent backend coupling.
elements.recommendation: Make the backend optional explicitly: skip the auth/world fetches when no VITE_BACKEND_URL is configured (a guard returning localStorage mode) so the offline path is intentional, not error-recovery. Verify the prod Vercel env sets VITE_BACKEND_URL if a backend is actually deployed.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA browser games degrade to offline cleanly without throwing network errors on the console at boot.

elements.name: ESLint config is intentionally narrow (NOT a rubber-stamp — KEEP)
elements.state: eslint.config.js (flat, v9) runs only no-undef + react/jsx-no-undef as errors, with a documented rationale: it's a crash-class tripwire for the orphaned-symbol bug that froze the game 3x during god-file extraction (iters 159/160/161). It deliberately does NOT enable jsx-key/no-unused-vars/no-console. So the React key-warning and dead-code items above are NOT caught by lint by design.
elements.recommendation: Keep the crash-class gate. CONSIDER adding react/jsx-key as 'error' (it would have caught the loot/XP-orb undefined-key class statically) and no-unused-vars as 'warn' — but only if the false-positive rate on the R3F/JSX surface is acceptable; test on the full src first.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA repos run jsx-key + exhaustive-deps + no-unused-vars in CI; adding jsx-key here would have prevented the key warning that shipped.

audits.summary: Crafty's world-purpose answer is structurally DIFFERENT from the reference: it is an infinite procedural voxel solo-frontier (Hearth at origin → deterministic landmark "shrines" → a fixed far-edge Blight Heart climax with the named Shadow Dragon boss), navigated by a genuinely strong HUD wayfinding rail (always-on ObjectiveTracker arrow + a rich Compass strip + in-world emissive beacons). That spine is real and shipped. But almost every element that makes worldofclaudecraft.com read FINISHED is ABSENT in Crafty: there is no town/hub (only a flat stone plinth with a brazier), no static NPCs (the lone "villager" is a randomly-wandering passive mob you press G to trade with), no quest-givers, no nametags, no combat log, no cooldown-sweep ability bar (CD timers exist in logic but are invisible), no framed unit/target portrait plate, a thin square desktop-only minimap that omits every destination it already tracks, no ambient life beyond generic wander, and quests are generic chores ("defeat 5 mobs") with no lore/giver/log threading them to the climax. Notably, this category's gap is ABSENCE OF CONTENT, not dead/legacy code — every world-purpose element that IS present is live and wired. Most items are net-new content that reuses existing seams (TradingInterface, claimQuest/QUEST_LIST, mobKillBus, compass marker data, voxelKit primitives, ECS entity.health), so effort concentrates in M/L authoring rather than engine work.

elements.name: Populated/functional HUB (town at the Hearth)
elements.state: No hub exists. world/homeAnchor.js stamps a flat 15x15 stone plinth (HEARTH_Y=56) at the world origin as the spawn floor; Terrain.jsx:443-456 adds ONLY a small brazier (stone bowl + glowing ember, capture-null) on it. There are no buildings, no town, no services, no vendors-in-place, no quest board. The only world structures anywhere are: the bare Hearth plinth, underground cobblestone Dungeon chambers (terrain.worker.js:185-244,461), and 2 deterministic landmark silhouettes (Spire tower / Sky-arch monument, landmarks.js + Terrain.jsx:477-511). No houses/chapel/stalls/tents/tavern/roads.
elements.recommendation: Build a real hub on the already-stamped Hearth plinth: timber/stone voxel buildings (a forge, a market stall, a quest hall), a warm-lit altar center, and roads radiating outward toward the shrine beacons. Reuse the voxelKit Cube/Emissive primitives already used for landmarks (Terrain.jsx:439+) and stamp it deterministically at chunk-gen like stampHomeAnchor (zero save data, capture-safe via the existing isCaptureMode gates). This single element anchors NPCs (K2), spawn-destinations (K10), and ambient life (K7) — highest leverage in the category.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA (worldofclaudecraft.com): you spawn in Eastbrook Vale, a market town with timber-framed houses, shingled roofs, a chapel, market stalls, tents, campfires, a mine portal, a fishing dock, and roads painted into the terrain. Crafty spawns you on an empty stone pad with a single brazier. Gap: an entire inhabited starting place.

elements.name: Static NPCs — vendors & quest-givers (fixed, interactive)
elements.state: No fixed NPCs. The only 'NPC' is the villager mob (mobTypes.js:12, passive, weight 0.6), RNG-spawned by the same SpawnerSystem ring as hostiles (SimplifiedNPCSystem.jsx:89-173) at random positions, wandering via generic passive-mob AI (ai.worker.js:317). Press G within 4u opens TradingInterface (InputManager.jsx:165-194 → ui/TradingInterface.jsx, a fixed 8-row barter/coin shop). It is UNBINDABLE (allegiance.js:10) with a green compass dot (HUD.jsx:522-545). There are ZERO quest-givers — quests auto-activate from QUEST_LIST (QuestSystem.jsx:21-52); you never talk to anyone.
elements.recommendation: Place a small set of STATIC named NPCs at fixed hub coords (not RNG mobs): a Merchant wired to the existing TradingInterface (trade logic + G-interact seam already work — just need a fixed anchor instead of a wandering villager), a Quest-Giver gating the existing claimQuest/QUEST_LIST, plus 1-2 flavor NPCs. Freeze their positions (skip the AIWorkerSystem tick), add an idle bob + an overhead '!'/'?' glyph. Reuse MobModel's villager render (MobModel.jsx:287-305). Cheapest path to the 'quest hub' feel since both backends exist.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: each hub has weapon/armorsmiths, a banker, and quest-givers with '!' markers at fixed townspots. Crafty has a randomly-wandering merchant mob and no quest-givers. Gap: fixed, role-distinct, interactive townsfolk + the quest-acquisition ritual.

elements.name: Entity nametags (floating overhead labels)
elements.state: ZERO nametags anywhere — verified by grep across all src (no nametag/Billboard/<Html>/Text3D/drei Text usage). MobModel.jsx renders only the voxel body; no overhead label, no per-entity health bar. The only over-entity health bar is the single BossHealthBar pinned to the top of the HUD (ui/BossHealthBar.jsx). Mobs/villagers are anonymous; only the boss is named ('Shadow Dragon', bossConfig.js:4) and it appears in toasts, never over the entity.
elements.recommendation: Add billboarded nametags over mobs/NPCs. Perf-safe path: a drei <Billboard> + pre-baked canvas/texture sprite (avoid <Html> for many mobs — DOM cost). Name + thin health bar for hostiles within ~30m, name-only for NPCs. Capture-suppress (like every overlay) to keep the 20 visual baselines byte-stable. Feed from a new display-name field in MOB_TYPES + the live entity.health/maxHealth already on the ECS entity.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: every unit has a tab-target nametag + health bar + threat/level coloring. Crafty shows nothing over any entity except the player's crosshair. Gap: the single biggest 'this thing has identity' cue.

elements.name: Combat log (scrolling damage/event feed)
elements.state: No combat log (grep: zero combatLog matches). Combat feedback is purely ephemeral: floating DamageNumber sprites in-world (combatVfx.jsx via SimplifiedNPCSystem.jsx:518) and transient NotificationStack toasts (QuestSystem.jsx:497, 4s auto-dismiss, loot/quest/achievement only). No persistent scrollable 'You hit X for N / X hit you / X died' record.
elements.recommendation: Add a small bottom-left scrolling combat log (reuse the Panel primitive). Clean event seam already exists: mobKillBus (game/mobKillBus.js, emitMobKill) + the damageMob path (SimplifiedNPCSystem.jsx:454) + damagePlayer. Pipe into a capped ring buffer (~8 lines), color-code crit/death, collapse on touch like QuestTracker, capture-suppress.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: a scrolling combat log with threat metrics + color-coded crit/heal/death lines (the WoCC creator specifically cited 'threat metrics in the combat log' as a polish surprise). Crafty has only ephemeral floating numbers. Gap: the readable record that makes combat legible.

elements.name: Cooldown sweeps (ability/spell CD radial on a bar)
elements.state: Cooldown LOGIC exists everywhere (dodge CD Components.jsx:164/896, beast-form transform CD:116, melee MELEE_COOLDOWN:244, per-form mult, spell mana gating) but there is NO cooldown VISUAL. The bottom hotbar (GameHud.jsx MinecraftHotbar) shows building blocks only, no CD overlay. The spell readout (HUD.jsx:631-637) is static name + mana-cost text. The left rail (GameHud.jsx:70-77) is panel-openers, not abilities. No radial wipe, no greyed-recharging state, no GCD swipe anywhere.
elements.recommendation: Build a real ability bar surfacing the signature Aspect abilities (GRAB/SNARE/IMBUE/ROAR/dodge — currently only HUD meters: HUD.jsx FerocityBar/KineticBar/SoulBar/ResonanceBar). Render each as a Slot with a CSS conic-gradient radial sweep driven by (now-cdStart)/duration in a rAF (Game-Loop-Isolation: ref/DOM write, no per-frame React state). CD timestamps already exist in store/refs. Add resource desaturation when unaffordable (mirror the existing 'ready' label logic).
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: action bar with cooldown sweeps + range/resource coloring + GCD swipe (WoCC README lists this). Crafty's cooldowns are fully implemented but invisible — abilities feel undiscoverable + un-juicy. Gap: the canonical 'my button is recharging' affordance.

elements.name: Framed unit/portrait plate (player + target frame)
elements.state: No unit frame / portrait. Player vitals are loose stacked bars top-left (HUD.jsx:617-625: Health/Mana/Hunger + Aspect meters) — no portrait, no name, no level chip, no frame. NO target frame at all (you never see a selected/looked-at enemy's name/health in a plate; only in-world floating DamageNumbers). Player is first-person (only render/playerRender.jsx StableMagicHands; no visible body → no portrait source). BeastAvatar (render/BeastAvatar.jsx) is a transient transform-reveal cam, not a persistent portrait.
elements.recommendation: Compose a framed player unit plate: a Panel with a static class/Aspect portrait icon (reuse the filled game-icons set), name/level, and the health/mana bars docked inside the frame instead of floating. Add a target plate that appears when looking at/near a mob (reuse checkMobsInMeleeCone / nearest-mob seam) showing target name + health — pairs naturally with nametags (K3). A rendered 3D portrait is overkill given first-person; an authored icon is the SOTA-adequate cheap path.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: portrait unit frames for self + target (+ party), buff/debuff bars with durations. Crafty has loose unframed bars + no target frame. Gap: the framed 'this is me / this is what I'm fighting' anchor.

elements.name: Real radial minimap (blips + landmarks + zone map)
elements.state: A minimap exists but is thin: HUD.jsx:182-265 — a 130px SQUARE canvas, top-down, redrawn every 250ms, showing a faint grid, mob dots (green=passive/red=hostile from store.mobEntities via MinimapSyncSystem.jsx:432), a white player triangle, an 'N' tick + raw x,z coords. DESKTOP-ONLY (HUD.jsx:664, hidden on touch). It does NOT show Hearth/home, shrines, the Blight Heart, chests, or biome — even though the Compass (HUD.jsx:337-577) already tracks all of those. No openable zone/world map.
elements.recommendation: Upgrade to a circular clipped minimap with persistent blips for HOME (origin), nearest SHRINE (nearestLandmark, shrines.js), BLIGHT HEART (blightHeartSite), chests, NPCs — all data already consumed by the compass, so this reuses existing seams. Add a north-rotating ring + a press-M full zone map (zoomed-out loaded-chunk view with all known markers). Give it a touch placement so it stops being desktop-only.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: minimap with blips (mobs, party, vendors) + a full openable zone map. Crafty's minimap shows only mob dots + self, omits every destination it already tracks, and is square + desktop-only. Gap: the minimap as a real navigation tool, plus an absent world/zone map.

elements.name: Ambient life / patrol routes (a world that moves on its own)
elements.state: No designed ambient life. All mobs (incl. villagers) share one behavior: ai.worker.js:317 'wandering logic for idle/passive mobs' (random target wander; hostiles aggro/chase). No patrol routes, no day/night NPC schedules, no herds/flocks, no idle animation beyond wander, no townsfolk-with-tasks. SquadAISystem (world/SquadAISystem.jsx) governs BOUND allies following the player, not ambient wildlife. The world is empty until SpawnerSystem rings mobs around the player; nothing lives at fixed places.
elements.recommendation: Add fixed-anchor ambient agents at the hub: townsfolk on short looping waypoint patrols (a path-follow over the wander AI), idle merchants, and passive critter herds (pigs/cows) grazing near spawn instead of popping in randomly. A lightweight schedule (day = townsfolk active, night = retreat during the siege) ties ambient life to the already-built day/night loop (useDayNightClock.js). Keep deterministic + capture-suppressed.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: camps, wildlife, bandits, NPCs at quest hubs, roads with traffic — the world reads inhabited before you act. Crafty's world is procedurally pretty but biologically empty until mobs spawn around you. Gap: self-driven life that makes the place feel alive.

elements.name: Legible narrative arc (quests beyond generic chores)
elements.state: A skeletal arc exists: the ObjectiveTracker (HUD.jsx:274-335) names a 2-beat spine 'Reach the frontier shrine' → 'Shatter the Blight Heart' (onboardingTips.js), and the climax is a fixed lair with the named Shadow Dragon (blightHeart.js, bossConfig.js). But quest CONTENT is generic chores: QUEST_LIST (QuestSystem.jsx:21-52) is 'defeat first mob / 5 mobs / place 20 blocks / cast 10 spells / 10 zombies...' with endless auto-scaling 'Bounty N: defeat 15+5n mobs' (makeRepeatableQuest:57). No quest text, lore, named givers, branching, or beats tie chores to the Blight Heart spine. Quests live in a 3-slot QuestTracker — there is NO full quest LOG (grep: zero QuestLog).
elements.recommendation: Author a short narrative chain threading the existing Hearth→shrine→Blight-Heart spine (e.g. 'The Hearthkeeper asks you to light the frontier shrines; each reveals a Blight fragment; the final shrine opens the lair'). Reuse the existing reach_shrine driver (QuestSystem.jsx:199-221) + shrine-chest reward seam — add flavor lore/giver fields to quest objects + a quest LOG panel (the AchievementsPanel modal is a ready template). Data + one panel, not new systems.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: ~60 quests + one named storyline (Gravecaller conspiracy → Korzul the Gravewyrm) with givers, dialog, and a quest log/world map. Crafty has a 2-line objective spine bolted over chore quests, no quest text, no givers, no log. Gap: the connective story that turns 'defeat 5 mobs' into 'why'.

elements.name: Visible destinations at spawn (the 40-second rule)
elements.state: Partially built — Crafty's strongest feels-complete surface. At spawn: the always-on ObjectiveTracker arrow naming the objective + live distance (HUD.jsx:274-335, full-circle), the Compass strip with HOME/SHRINE/BLIGHT HEART/BOSS markers (HUD.jsx:337-577), and in-world emissive beacon crowns on landmark Spires/arches (Terrain.jsx:477-511, #46E0FF/#F5D76E, raised to clear fog at Y56+) + the Blight Heart monolith beacon (Terrain.jsx:516+). BUT the actual spawn-visible silhouette depends on the deterministic landmark hash (~1.4% of chunks, landmarks.js:18) — there is NO guarantee a shrine is in view at spawn; the plan's S10 'guaranteed spawn-visible silhouette / 40-second rule' is planned, not confirmed shipped. The Hearth has only a brazier, not a 'you are here' framing.
elements.recommendation: Guarantee a destination silhouette is visible from the Hearth: either force-place the nearest shrine landmark within the spawn view frustum (deterministic near-origin override), or raise/brighten the first shrine's beacon so it clears terrain from the plinth. Pair with the hub build (K1) so spawn shows BOTH a place (town) and a goal (distant beacon). The compass/ObjectiveTracker rails are already excellent — the gap is purely guaranteeing the in-world silhouette, not the HUD cue.
elements.sotaGap: SOTA: you spawn in a town and immediately see roads to named hubs + a pass to the next zone. Crafty has best-in-class HUD wayfinding but no guaranteed in-world destination silhouette at spawn and no town to spawn into. Gap: closing the last mile between 'the HUD says go there' and 'I can SEE there from where I stand'.

deadOrLegacyOrError: No dead/legacy/error code found WITHIN Category K — every world-purpose element present is live and wired. The category's defining problem is ABSENCE OF CONTENT, not dead code.

frontend/src/world/landmarks.js:6 — LANDMARK_TYPES=2 + landmarkTypeAt only ever produce Spire/Sky-arch; the 'shrine' naming in shrines.js/HUD/quests is an ALIAS over these 2 generic visuals — no distinct shrine model exists (naming-vs-asset drift, not dead code; a shrine reads as a plain tower/arch).

frontend/src/game/mobTypes.js:12 — the villager 'NPC' is mechanically identical to a passive animal (wander AI, same spawner, no name, no fixed home); it is functional but mis-cast as world-content (an NPC implemented as a mob).
Out of scope for K: the prompt's referenced dead 'gameStarted' flag and legacy 'CRAFTY RPG' ESC menu overlay are menu/state-category items, not world-purpose elements — not enumerated here.
topPriorities: HUB at the Hearth (K1, REBUILD, L/critical) — the highest-leverage lever; the origin plinth is already stamped + flat and voxelKit primitives already render structures, and a hub anchors NPCs + quest-givers + spawn-destination + ambient life all at once.

Static vendor + quest-giver NPCs (K2, REBUILD, M/high) — the TradingInterface + claimQuest/QUEST_LIST backends ALREADY work; just re-anchor to fixed hub coords instead of a wandering RNG villager. Cheapest path to the 'quest hub' feel.

Entity nametags + target frame (K3+K6, IMPROVE, M/high) — zero exist today; the single biggest 'this is an RPG' legibility cue, and the two share one implementation over data (entity.health/maxHealth) that already exists.

Cooldown sweeps + a real ability bar (K5, IMPROVE, M/high) — all CD timers are implemented in logic but invisible; surface the Aspect signature abilities (GRAB/SNARE/IMBUE/ROAR/dodge) as a bar with conic-gradient radial sweeps over existing timestamps.

Narrative quest chain over the existing shrine→Blight-Heart spine (K9, IMPROVE→content, L/high) — the objective spine + reach_shrine driver + shrine-chest rewards already exist; add lore/giver fields + a quest LOG panel to turn generic chores into a 'why'.
